L1.area: Repository file upload and content management

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists in the repository file upload functionality. On Windows, a path validation check can be bypassed by appending a trailing dot to a restricted directory name, allowing an attacker to upload crafted configuration files into that directory. This enables arbitrary command execution on the server when repository operations are performed.

L3.area: Repository file upload and content management
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists in the repository file upload functionality. On Windows, a path validation check can be bypassed by appending a trailing dot to a restricted directory name, allowing an attacker to upload crafted configuration files into that directory. This enables arbitrary command execution on the server when repository operations are performed.

ground_truth.reason: The path validation function fails to account for Windows treating a trailing dot as equivalent to the original directory name, allowing attackers to bypass the upload restriction and place a crafted config file that enables arbitrary command execution via SSH access.
